steps.title: Identify the triangle and known sides
steps.content:
The figure shows a right triangle with hypotenuse labeled 14 and an acute angle of 58°. Segment AB is the side opposite this angle.
We need to find its length.

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio
steps.content:
The sine of an angle in a right triangle equals the length of the opposite side divided by the length of the hypotenuse.
Here $\theta = 58^\circ$, the hypotenuse is 14, and the opposite side is AB.
Substitute into the ratio: $$\sin 58^\circ = \frac{AB}{14}$$.
Multiply both sides by 14: $$AB = 14 \sin 58^\circ$$.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
